title: Insulin
body_html:
Insulin and glucose transport
  • Glucose needs to be transported across the phospholipid bilayer into the cell so that it can be utilised (either for energy via ATP or stored as glycogen)
  • The phospholipid bilayer is virtually impermeable to glucose normally
  • Insulin binds to receptors on the cell and induces GLUT4 proteins
  • These glucose transporter proteins embed into the phospholipid bilayer and act like tunnels through which glucose can pass along its concentration gradient
  • This is an example of facilitated diffusion

3
0
